hero.eyebrow: ZIAN AI CONCEPTS — AI · Creative Tech · Engineering
hero.headline: Designing intelligence.
hero.sub: I build interfaces, agents and creative tools at the intersection of AI and motion — a one-person studio shipping work that thinks, reacts, and feels alive.

lines: Software should feel like it's thinking with you,
Every pixel, every motion, every model decision
is a chance to make machines feel human.

work.title: Things I've built lately.
work.intro: A rotating selection of recent experiments, products and commissioned work — across AI agents, generative interfaces and creative R&D.
work.endLine: Full case studies on request — drop me a line.

capabilities.title: What I do.
capabilities.intro: Engineering and design rolled into one practice — I move from research to production without handoffs.

items.title: AI engineering
items.body: Agents, RAG systems, evals, and production LLM pipelines built with Claude, GPT and open models.

items.title: Creative coding
items.body: WebGL, shaders, generative graphics and motion systems — performant, expressive, original.

items.title: Concept & strategy
items.body: From first idea to working prototype — naming, narrative, system thinking and product framing.

items.title: Interaction design
items.body: Interfaces that respond, breathe, and feel intentional. Detail-obsessed micro-interactions and motion craft.

cta.label: Sounds like your project? Let's talk.

process.title: How I work.

steps.title: Frame
steps.body: We start with the real question — not the deliverable. I dig into the problem, the constraints and the people involved.

steps.title: Sketch
steps.body: Working prototypes within days, not weeks. I build in the browser, in code, with real data — never in slides.

steps.title: Sharpen
steps.body: Iterate against reality. Real users, real performance budgets, real tradeoffs. Cut everything that doesn't earn its place.

steps.title: Ship
steps.body: Production-grade craft from day one. Then maintain, measure, evolve. Software is a relationship, not a delivery.

process.intro: Four phases, no waterfall — iterative, direct, shippable from week one.

connect.intro: Talk to the AI — or leave a trace. Both reach me.
connect.inlineFormPrompt: Drop your email and the gist — I'll get back to you within 24 hours.

suggestions: What does ZIAN AI CONCEPTS do?
Which AI models do you use?
What sets you apart from an agency?
aiDemo.disclaimer: Questions are processed locally and never stored.
aiDemo.liveDisclaimer: Prompts are sent directly to the Ollama endpoint, never to third parties.

replies: ZIAN AI CONCEPTS is a one-person studio for AI engineering, creative coding and interaction design. I build interfaces, agents and generative systems — from concept to production.
A collaboration typically starts with a short kick-off call. We then iterate closely: I deliver working prototypes fast rather than long specification documents.
I mainly use Claude by Anthropic and open-source models via Ollama — depending on the use-case. For production applications I prefer Claude for its reliability and reasoning strength.
Unlike an agency, there are no account managers or handoffs. You work directly with me — meaning faster decisions, less overhead and more product coherence.
mock.fallback: Great question! I'm André Zimmermann, AI engineer and designer at ZIAN AI CONCEPTS. Feel free to reach out directly if you'd like to know more.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.body:
Note: This is an English courtesy translation. The German version on /de/datenschutz is the legally binding text.

Last updated: May 2026

1. Controller
The data controller within the meaning of the GDPR is:
André Zimmermann
[Street + Number]
[ZIP City]
Germany
Email: hello@andrezimmermann.dev

2. Website Access
When you access this website, the hosting provider Vercel Inc. (340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, USA) automatically logs information in server logfiles: IP address, date and time of the request, transferred data volume, browser and operating system, referrer URL. This data is retained for a maximum of 14 days and used exclusively for the technical operation of the website and to mitigate attacks (Art. 6 (1)(f) GDPR — legitimate interest). Vercel is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework; a data processing agreement with standard contractual clauses is in place.

3. Contact Form
When you use the contact form in the “Connect” section, the data you enter (name optional, email address, message) is transmitted to me for the purpose of responding to your inquiry and possible follow-up questions. Processing is performed via Formspree Inc. (251 Little Falls Drive, Wilmington, DE 19808, USA) on the basis of Art. 6 (1)(b) and (f) GDPR. A data processing agreement with Formspree is in place. The data is stored for the duration of the business relationship and deleted at the latest six months after the inquiry is resolved, provided no statutory retention obligations apply.

4. Reach Analysis with Plausible Analytics
This website uses Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly analytics service operated by Plausible Insights OÜ (Västriku tn 2, 50403 Tartu, Estonia). Plausible sets no cookies and collects no personal data. All measurements are recorded fully anonymized on EU-based servers and stored in aggregate form. Re-identification of individual visitors is technically impossible. Legal basis: consent (Art. 6 (1)(a) GDPR), which you grant via the analytics banner and may revoke at any time with effect for the future.

5. Cookies and Local Storage
This website sets no tracking cookies, no advertising cookies, and no third-party cookies. Two purely functional entries are stored in your browser's local storage (localStorage): your language preference and your decision on the analytics banner. This data never leaves your browser.

6. Data Recipients
Data is transmitted only to the processors named in this declaration (Vercel, Formspree, Plausible). No transmission to third parties for other purposes takes place.

7. Your Rights
You have the right at any time to:
· Access the data stored about you (Art. 15 GDPR)
· Rectification of inaccurate data (Art. 16 GDPR)
· Erasure (“right to be forgotten”, Art. 17 GDPR)
· Restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
· Data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
· Object to processing (Art. 21 GDPR)
· Withdraw consent with effect for the future (Art. 7 (3) GDPR)
· Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR)

To exercise your rights, an informal email to hello@andrezimmermann.dev is sufficient.

8. Changes to This Privacy Policy
This privacy policy will be updated when processing operations or the legal framework change. The current version is always available on this page.
